name: µVCA II
description: Dual DC-coupled VCA with continuously variable linear-to-exponential response per channel. CV attenuator, bias knob for above-unity gain, cascaded mixing output when OUT A is unpatched.

controls.name: CV A
controls.description: Attenuates the CV A input before it sums with BIAS A.
controls.detail: CCW: zero · CW: unity · LED tracks resulting CV level

controls.name: BIAS A
controls.description: Sets initial gain of amp A before CV is applied. Max bias = 5V = gain of 1.
controls.detail: Use as manual level when no CV patched · summed with CV A

controls.name: EXP-LIN A
controls.description: Blends response curve between linear and exponential.
controls.detail: CCW: linear (audio VCA feel) · CW: exponential (CV/envelope feel)

controls.name: CV B
controls.description: CV attenuator for channel B. Identical to CV A.

controls.name: BIAS B
controls.description: Manual level / bias offset for amp B.
controls.detail: 5V = unity gain · summed with CV B

controls.name: EXP-LIN B
controls.description: Blends channel B response between linear and exponential.

inputs.name: CV A
inputs.description: Level CV for amp A, summed with BIAS A. Voltages above 5V give above-unity gain

inputs.name: IN A
inputs.description: Signal input for amp A. DC-coupled so audio or CV can pass

inputs.name: CV B
inputs.description: Level CV for amp B, summed with BIAS B

inputs.name: IN B
inputs.description: Signal input for amp B. DC-coupled

outputs.name: OUT A
outputs.description: Amp A output. When unpatched, signal is normalized into OUT B for cascaded mixing

outputs.name: OUT B
outputs.description: Amp B output. Sums OUT A when OUT A is unpatched — acts as simple 2-channel mixer output

behaviors.name: Cascaded Mixer Mode
behaviors.description: With nothing plugged into OUT A, amp A's signal is internally normalized into OUT B. Two VCA channels become a CV-controlled 2-channel mixer from a single jack.

behaviors.name: Above-Unity Gain
behaviors.description: Both BIAS and CV sum; voltages above 5V drive gain past 1. Useful for reviving weak signals, boosting envelope shapes, or fattening up synth outs before a filter.

behaviors.name: Continuous Linear/Exp Blend
behaviors.description: Sweep smoothly between linear (audio-accurate level control) and exponential (envelope-following that feels punchy). The middle region is a subtle response that works well as a DC-coupled CV attenuator.

behaviors.name: DC-Coupled Signal Path
behaviors.description: CV can be passed through the amp as easily as audio. Scale envelopes, attenuate pitch sequences, or amplitude-modulate one CV with another — this is a VCA that doubles as a utility processor.

patchIdeas.name: Classic envelope + audio VCA
patchIdeas.patch: VCO into IN A · envelope into CV A · EXP-LIN A fully clockwise for expo response · BIAS A at zero · punchy plucked note shaping
patchIdeas.diagram:
µVCA II:
* EXP-LIN A=CW (expo) · BIAS A=0

- VCO (Out) -> µVCA II (IN A)
- Envelope (Out) >> µVCA II (CV A)
- µVCA II (OUT A) -> Output (In) // expo VCA pluck

patchIdeas.name: Two-source mixer via cascade
patchIdeas.patch: VCO 1 into IN A · VCO 2 into IN B · leave OUT A unpatched · take sum at OUT B · BIAS knobs set mix levels · CV inputs act as individual automation for each source
patchIdeas.diagram:
µVCA II:
* BIAS A noon · BIAS B noon · OUT A unpatched

- VCO 1 (Out) -> µVCA II (IN A)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> µVCA II (IN B)
- LFO (Out) >> µVCA II (CV A)
- µVCA II (OUT B) -> Filter (In) // cascaded mix of A+B

patchIdeas.name: Dual-envelope AM
patchIdeas.patch: VCO into IN A · LFO sine into IN B · envelope on CV A for amplitude shape · slower LFO on CV B modulates the modulator · OUT A out OUT B provides tremolo layer · ring-mod-like amplitude interaction
patchIdeas.diagram:
µVCA II:
* EXP-LIN A=LIN · EXP-LIN B=LIN

- VCO (Out) -> µVCA II (IN A)
- Audio LFO (Out) -> µVCA II (IN B)
- Envelope (Out) >> µVCA II (CV A)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> µVCA II (CV B)
- µVCA II (OUT A) -> Mixer (In 1)
- µVCA II (OUT B) -> Mixer (In 2) // AM layering

patchIdeas.name: CV scaler for sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer CV into IN A (DC-coupled) · BIAS A at zero · CV A attenuator sets scaling · EXP-LIN at linear · use OUT A as pre-scaled pitch CV to a second VCO for tracking offsets
patchIdeas.diagram:
µVCA II:
* EXP-LIN A=LIN (for clean CV) · BIAS A=0

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> µVCA II (IN A)
- Offset Source (Out) >> µVCA II (CV A)
- µVCA II (OUT A) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct) // scaled pitch CV

patchIdeas.name: Side-chain ducking
patchIdeas.patch: Pad into IN A · inverted envelope from kick drum trigger into CV A · BIAS A around 3 V so pad sits at ~unity when nothing plays · each kick briefly ducks the pad · classic dance-floor pump
patchIdeas.diagram:
µVCA II:
* EXP-LIN A=LIN · BIAS A ~3V

- Pad (Out) -> µVCA II (IN A)
- Kick Trigger (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Inverter (In)
- Inverter (Out) >> µVCA II (CV A)
- µVCA II (OUT A) -> Output (In) // side-chain duck
